Nice.

Not too bad.
Just cranking away.

I feel you.
Totally feel you.
How about y'all?
How are you guys doing?
Good, good.
I'm going on vacation on Saturday, so I'm not going to be here next week.
It's my first serious time off since I joined Growthx, so I'm just trying to get all my ducks in a row and make sure that there are no surprises while I'm away.
Okay.
Yeah, huge.
But it is already ahead of stuff.
It feels really good.
Not everyone's like that.
I wouldn't be like that.
So it's really nice to have that on our end.
Hey, Matt.
Are we waiting for Sakshi or maybe it's too late for her?
We can move without she had something come up this morning, so she may make it if she has an opportunity, but she's not.
Okay, perfect.
So before we get into the content and like the more detailed stuff, I logged into Webflow and I, you know, I know Sakshi, she shared like walkthrough video that I think your web agency shared.

So I'm thinking that it's probably something to do with the user permissions.
Yeah.
Beau, can you help accelerate this point potentially?
I know that the line with Axio is a little bit discombobulated to a certain degree, but I think we need to just, like, run this so that we can help facilitate this work done.
Thank you.
Yes, let me contact them.
What is the username?

So I'm going to share it in the channel.
Thank you.
Let me do the chat.
Thank you.
Awesome.
Yeah.
Okay.
Perfect.
Just wanted to get that out of the way.
So today there are a couple of things I wanted to show you, but I first wanted to see if you had any other feedback on the calibration articles.
I promised you they'd be done today.
That's a little tiny bit delayed.
And that's only because I sent them over to a guy on our team named Keith, who is very, he actually, I guess, helped copyright some of our stuff.
But he's like a really good person to review for the overall content and not messaging, but the content.
And so he said he's happy to do it.
He should have it today or tomorrow.
That's what he said, just based off of his timelines.
Okay.
So that's, that's the update.
And so if we want to give it like, I'll make sure that it's, it's all in there and that all of our stuff is fine.
Okay, okay, that makes sense.
Thank you for your patience.
The truth is I thought somebody had sent it to him and nobody had sent it to him.
So I'll take that, but yeah.
Okay, okay, no problem.
We are working on eight new blogs right now, but depending on his feedback, we can go back and like make some changes.
I mean, the documents that you shared, was able to kind of plug them into Aerofs and we were able to get, I think the product messaging should be on point.

Obviously, you know, make sure to flag anything that sounds, you don't want to emphasize, you know, some clients, their product might have certain features, but they don't want to always talk about them for, you know, various reasons.
So you can just flag that stuff as it comes up.
Sounds good.
Okay, perfect.
So today...
I wanted to show you your looker report.
So for each and every one of our clients, we do performance reporting.
And as we publish blogs, every week when we meet up, we review the performance report.
We take a look at like if there are any changes, any blogs are doing particularly well, or if any blogs are getting less traffic.

And because we publish a lot, so we typically publish like at least six or seven blogs per week, ideally, finding a way to track the performance just of those blogs is a little bit tricky because clients usually have like you have your own blog posts.
And like, we want to make sure that we're just tracking our efforts.
First and foremost, you have like a good idea of like whether like the stuff's making an impact.
So we do that using a looker studio report.
This is, this is
So I already, I added the link to it in the agenda.
You all should have access to it and you have editing access.
That means that you can like adjust the timelines, things like that.
But if you feel like you really want to make some big changes, just let us know so that I'm not, I don't think that like something changed overnight, basically.
So right now, this report contains the data from your entire website because we haven't published anything yet.
But once we start publishing, we're going to use this filter right here to filter out all of our just our blog posts.
And this chart right here, it's showing the data from your Google Search Console.
So that's the data source for this particular for this particular graph.
And then this table is also pulling data from your Google Search Console.
So you can see like URL clicks, impressions, overall click-through rate.
We can also modify this table so that it shows, like, week-over-week performance.
Like, you can see whether a blog is doing better or worse, so you can see, like, changes in traffic.
The next page, so it's three pages, the next page combines data from Google Analytics 4, which is this table right here.
And this is GSC data.
This is just the query data, so it helps to know, like, what type of queries are, like, driving the most traffic to your site.
Obviously, this is just your entire website at the moment.
We're also going to filter this out.
And then the last page of the report is LLM referral traffic, so this page basically has a filter on it that it only includes referral traffic from LLM, such as, like, Chachi.
Gemini, Perplexity, Deepseek.
So you get like a good, typically we don't filter this page for our blog posts, as a lot of clients just want to see the impact on their entire site from LLMs.
So this is where you're going to be able to see that.
This pie chart right here, it's incorrect right now.
I'm going to have to fix it later because it should be showing Chachi PT, Gemini.
It should show like a breakdown between different LLMs, but I'm going to tackle this later because I really couldn't figure it out in time.
So it's great to have this data.
Thanks for pulling that.
No problem.
Quick question on the query strings in there for the search from the search console.
I think it's on the weekly performance.
It's GA4 potentially because it was the.
Yeah.
OK.
So.

Oh, is the query below?
I see.
OK.
Yeah.
So down below is going to be who's searching.
I just I was looking at this on these are all the landing pages right now, which I also want to see.
So please don't feel like you have to get rid of those because that's something I look at frequently.
I was just wondering how we were looking at that to identify what they were searching for.
But it looks like if we scroll down to the next section of this, that's the query.
Yeah.
of right now, we kill it when people search for our name.
Yeah.
So this is branded search traffic.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Like a lot of people are interested in jobs at Flosum.
Yeah.
Yeah.
But yeah, sometimes I mean, the query.
These are interesting because I'll give you an example.

And they didn't have any content on their website.
So we literally created a blog post that was just like a compilation of different reviews.
I mean, they're like consumer facing, so it's different.
And now they're like capturing that traffic that otherwise would be wasted.
So it's good to check in once in a while and see if there are any opportunities for...

there's a thunderstorm here.

Yeah.
I appreciate that.

So I believe it's no.

I believe it's a no from my Webflow experience.

you.
Sorry to interrupt.

Yeah, thank you.
Well, Aida, I'm unmuted.
Just a couple things on the reports.
One, Matt, I also like when I do this internally, like the time over time and I think month over month, you know, when we zoom forward, a couple months makes a ton of sense.
That's where I usually start to look.

And then if we get, you know, 612 beyond, we'll start looking quarters, obviously, but with like the amount of traffic and then how this should be growing, I think month over month will make the most sense.
Just we'll zoom forward a little bit.

So that could be useful.
And then the only other addition is, I know we're starting, obviously, with new content.
I think that's the.

That should be the bulk as we've kind of discussed, but as we start to do refreshes too, we'll want to separate those and we've got a couple different ways to report on those, but since they will have some impressions and some traffic existing, know, we'll still want to separate that because like the moment we refresh, we haven't necessarily changed anything on their metrics.
We want to see that over time, but we'll, so we'll take a look at that separately.
There's a little bit different reporting, but just wanted to call that out that this is specific to the new content.
So, yes, totally.
I'm on board with that.
just for, I guess, a little bit of clarity from my end, since we've all, everyone on this has only worked with me for a month.
You all started at the exact same time working with me.
There are a lot of those leading indicators that are nice to look at for the weeklies, but there's so many of them that it doesn't make a lick of difference.
Like, I'll say this to Drew and Beau, like, don't ever expect me to ask you how many demos we have week over week because I can't.
It's just a silly number.
And so like it's sort of the same way I see with these is week over week might be good up front just so we can see how it goes so we can understand what's what's working short term.
But as we get into it monthly, it's just it's going to be perfect.
So especially with all other variables being considered, like once we get into the rhythm of, yes, it's X number every week, it's X number every week, it's X, it's just going to be that.

Do we see impressions?
And so we should see like batch of content within a certain number of days.
Like then we'll see us like impressions because stuff is starting.
Right.
And so as long as that's there, that for me, like that's how I always choose.
We have we're seeing growth and we zoom back a little bit and we can slowly zoom back as like the timeline extends a little bit.
But as long as it is going up more at the monthly level and then I lost my train of thought.
But that's exactly how I think about it.
then, yeah, it's growing.
We'll see those spikes.
But then if we're looking week over week, we see a 10 percent.
Traffic drop when we have 100 clicks, like that doesn't matter compared to the longer term, like impression growth.
As long as we're seeing that and keywords move into the right position, that's more of an indicator of future traffic than, you know, the week to week, small changes.
So totally on board.
I just wanted to echo it and plus one.
Yeah.
I have a question.
So as we're starting to see more and more growth here and more and more visitors, et cetera, can you guys provide on a weekly basis a list of domains and unique visitors?
Would that be possible?
You mean, like the, what do you mean by domains, like page URLs?
No.
So Flosum, for instance.
Well, people from the company, a number of people, and it can be more than three.
OK, and I don't want anybody who went to employment pages or anything, you know, I don't want people who are looking for a job.
What I'm looking for is I'm looking for a number of people, unique people from a domain that is more than three.
It's of like intent-ish data.
Yeah, but we don't have that capability in our, like, generally...
Well, Looker, you don't have that capability.
So we're, and Looker is just pulling in, like, reframing the data from GCNGA4.
So we, we're, we're just taking the data you all have access to already and gave us and putting it into a way that's more accessible and doable for our purposes.
So we're not adding, we're not supplementing with any...

I do think that's something that everybody wants more of and would be theoretically part of some offering in the future that we have, but it's definitely not what we're doing with this, and it's not even in our tech right now.
So maybe more of an add-on from another tool and how that integrates with what we're doing.
Yeah, I was asking because I'm building basically something that provides data from those coming to our site with intent.
So yeah, I'll reveal it later on, but this is a project I'm working on this quarter.
Awesome.

Well, we would love to see what comes out of that and what is available and comes from this type of content, because that will only make what we're choosing for topics and what conversion we do on those pages more impactful.
So collaboration on the other direction would be very helpful.
Yeah, absolutely.
And that's why I was asking you if you guys had that, because then we can do the comparison.
But we'll figure it out and have this conversation later.
I didn't want to interrupt or anything, but I was just curious.
Thank you.
No problem.
Yeah, I know some clients have used Koala in the past for that type of tracking, but it depends.
Another thing I wanted to talk about, I mean, I couldn't access, once I get access to like the actual blog, I'm going to be able to see like the layout better.
One thing I did notice, and I want to kind of talk about it early, I know that we're tracking like organic traffic for like the first couple of months as we're working together.
But I didn't want to, I didn't want to ask if, for example, your blog has, if you have like any custom CTAs that you could like plug into the blog posts.
Like not just, for example, here we have like the newsletter sign up and then we have like like a CTA at the top to like talk to an expert and a phone call.

But like sometimes we might have, depending on like the customer's CMS, we might be able to plug in like a CTA here, like check out the product page for data backup and archive or to download an e-book or something.
So is that something that like your CMS supports?
Yes, CMS supports that.
That's something that we haven't done and we haven't kept up with.
But yes, CMS can definitely support it.
The other thing that we can do is we can also insert a banner, a relevant one, like read the case study or something of that sort.
It just breaks away from so much text.
That's also something that we can do through Webflow, but that's a capability that we haven't really explored at all.
Okay.
Okay, perfect.
Yeah, with my experience with Webflow, that sounds like that's similar to what I've done at Scribe.
I've been...
I've been...
been...
Other places where I've used it.
We could also, Aida brings up a great point, and we could then consider over time, should we have, you know, what's on that right side area?
How should we be using that?
Like, we would want to look at, like, are we converting to newsletters and what happens downstream?
Like, obviously, we're just trying to generate traffic right now and get people onto the site so we can learn and do more things.

But I think over time, we should be analyzing that will be part of like a collaboration with us will be only, we'll get more impact by having that collaboration with you on the conversion front, step in line.
think we can probably have some of this already, but can be done with short codes or design on the, you know, banner over the top and stuff.
So I think that'll be a lot of future discussion, especially once we have a lot of traffic will be spent there and how to better utilize that traffic.
So, but good that you have a lot of those mechanisms in place.
We just have to utilize them a little bit more.
Yeah, we do have clarity in place as well.
And we did notice that people actually tend to go on the right a lot.
So we would think that we'd probably put insert a banner right down.
An e-book or something of that sort.
We haven't done any of it.
It's just the insights that we've collected, but we haven't re-reactioned on that.
It has to be upfront there.

Just when we were transitioning to this website, we thought that we were transitioning to Webflow so that we can incorporate all of that, but it never came to that point that we would incorporate all of this.

So, yeah, in case you were analyzing any of it and in case you think that there is something relevant, we can always supplement it with that clarity database of what the heat maps are saying and what everything, like what the recordings are saying.
And seeing if there's any relevant keywords or if there's any relevant banners that we can insert anywhere.
Yeah, Webflow is a little bit funky depending on, like, you all have so much tech and operations that I think we will overcome this better than places I've been before.
But because, like, you have one collection for the blog and typically you have one design for that.
And then how do you make that custom or how do you make, you know, get the right CTA because we're going to have content for different types of people with different problems.
So the one CTA won't necessarily fit all in the way that some other.
Blogs will.
So it'll be something to figure out and think on.
But with, like, again, the tech stack you're mentioning, there's a lot we can do in action.
So we'll start to put thoughts together on that and then collaborate on how to, like, actually execute that, given all the tech you do have.
But that's all good to know.
If I can't do anything, we can always do the text once.
So we could actually incorporate it in the text.
Yeah, yeah, absolutely.
So those are all the updates that I had.
So in case someone joined later, I am going to be out of office all of next week.
The content is going to be produced and delivered.
And also, if you decide, like, if you approve something for publishing, we will also publish it next week.
So there's a CM that's going to do that.
For any sort of questions or comments, you can reach out to Jakub.
He's like the right person, pretty much, like for everything.
That you want to know.
Aida, I just wanted to know, there were a bunch of documents that I'd sent across.
Were they helpful?
Do you need something more?
They were very helpful.
Yes, thank you.
We were talking about it in the beginning of the meeting.
I was able to use them and then I kind of shortened them a little bit, like just extracted like the key points and I added them to our polishing instructions.
So now I think the product messaging is a lot clearer.
Also, we incorporated like, I think it was like what the customer is looking for, like their main pain points.
So this is also what we incorporated into the polishing instructions.
And also I, for this week, we have an article that compares like that.
It's about own backup alternatives or actually own alternatives.
And I, for

There was a lot of valuable stuff in that doc regarding OWN and how it's different from Flosum, but I was a little bit hesitant to include it because some clients don't want to talk badly about their competitors, at least in a blog post where it's like everyone can see.
So I ended up removing some of it just to kind of tone it down.
Yeah, I understand.
Okay, perfect.
Good time.
You got that insight.
It's Bubba.
Thank you so much.
Yeah, yeah, no problem.
Thank you for sharing that.
Only one other item to you, so apologies to go off script, but the no index pages, kind of that cleanup, that has not been forgotten.
I started it.
I just need to marry in some of like the data from real pages so I can make more recommendations on where to go.
Sometimes it's very simple, like we should just redirect here.
Other times getting a larger scope of all of your pages is more difficult.
So I'm still in that process.
So I'll have more next week.
I don't know with the number of pages.
The is about off.
I'll be able to get through that entire list within the next week, but have more that we can start to action and start to whittle that down.
Probably be a couple week project would be my guess, but thanks for the patience on that as I was coming back from vacation, just didn't get to it as much as maybe I would have been another week.
So keep you updated on that one.
Okay, perfect.
Anybody else?
Any questions?
Nope.

I was just going to say that internally, if it makes more sense that way, it could be good for us to do a competitive where it's one of those best data backup and archive tools that lists all of them, and then it's not a bashing on them.
It's more of the comparison.
So I don't know which team that would be on, but we can talk about that.
I think overall, you'd want the total best list and any variants on that.
Then you want your alternatives.

to own backup, then you want the, you know, own backup versus Flosum, if that makes sense, like, and for all of your type of lists, and then also like any kind of synonyms to that larger original list, if there's anything that people call it that's not like the category name, like you'd see at G2, and so that's my typical play when you're looking at the MoFu kind of down software plays, like there's so much meat on the bone, and usually we can find more than the instincts say up front, so I agree with that, but you can, you can like not bash while still being honest, and leading the reader to why you are the better option, where, you know, what they truly need, and setting up, setting the table for that post, so we do that too, but a good call.
Okay, perfect, yeah, thank you so much for your time, looking forward to seeing the feedback, and yeah, any questions, just send me a message in Slack.
Thank you, see you next week.
Thanks everyone.
you.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly sync between Flosum and GrowthX to discuss content strategy, performance reporting, and next steps for blog content creation and optimization.](https://fathom.video/share/iCjVVTjnQtoV357jvPDcjzvRGhT8aXiV?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [GrowthX set up a Looker Studio report for tracking blog performance, including GSC and GA4 data](https://fathom.video/share/iCjVVTjnQtoV357jvPDcjzvRGhT8aXiV?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)
  - [Flosum's Webflow CMS supports custom CTAs and banners, offering opportunities for content optimization](https://fathom.video/share/iCjVVTjnQtoV357jvPDcjzvRGhT8aXiV?tab=summary&timestamp=1103.0)
  - [GrowthX is working on no-index page cleanup and will provide updates in the coming weeks](https://fathom.video/share/iCjVVTjnQtoV357jvPDcjzvRGhT8aXiV?tab=summary&timestamp=1473.0)
  - [Flosum team to review and provide feedback on calibration articles by end of week](https://fathom.video/share/iCjVVTjnQtoV357jvPDcjzvRGhT8aXiV?tab=summary&timestamp=201.0)

## Topics

### Performance Reporting Setup

  - [Looker Studio report created to track blog performance](https://fathom.video/share/iCjVVTjnQtoV357jvPDcjzvRGhT8aXiV?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)
  - [Includes data from Google Search Console and Google Analytics 4](https://fathom.video/share/iCjVVTjnQtoV357jvPDcjzvRGhT8aXiV?tab=summary&timestamp=400.0)
  - [Features filters to isolate GrowthX-created content](https://fathom.video/share/iCjVVTjnQtoV357jvPDcjzvRGhT8aXiV?tab=summary&timestamp=392.0)
  - [Tracks metrics like clicks, impressions, CTR, and LLM referral traffic](https://fathom.video/share/iCjVVTjnQtoV357jvPDcjzvRGhT8aXiV?tab=summary&timestamp=400.0)
  - [Team discussed preference for monthly vs. weekly performance tracking as content matures](https://fathom.video/share/iCjVVTjnQtoV357jvPDcjzvRGhT8aXiV?tab=summary&timestamp=662.0)

### Content Creation and Optimization

  - [GrowthX working on 8 new blog posts](https://fathom.video/share/iCjVVTjnQtoV357jvPDcjzvRGhT8aXiV?tab=summary&timestamp=253.0)
  - [Flosum team to review calibration articles and provide feedback](https://fathom.video/share/iCjVVTjnQtoV357jvPDcjzvRGhT8aXiV?tab=summary&timestamp=201.0)
  - [Discussion on incorporating custom CTAs and banners in blog posts](https://fathom.video/share/iCjVVTjnQtoV357jvPDcjzvRGhT8aXiV?tab=summary&timestamp=1124.0)
  - [Potential to leverage Clarity heatmap data for optimizing content layout](https://fathom.video/share/iCjVVTjnQtoV357jvPDcjzvRGhT8aXiV?tab=summary&timestamp=1253.0)
  - [Consideration for creating competitive comparison content without direct competitor bashing](https://fathom.video/share/iCjVVTjnQtoV357jvPDcjzvRGhT8aXiV?tab=summary&timestamp=1528.0)

### Technical Access and Permissions

  - [GrowthX team needs proper access to Webflow for blog post creation](https://fathom.video/share/iCjVVTjnQtoV357jvPDcjzvRGhT8aXiV?tab=summary&timestamp=682.0)
  - [Beau to adjust Webflow permissions to editor-level access (not design access)](https://fathom.video/share/iCjVVTjnQtoV357jvPDcjzvRGhT8aXiV?tab=summary&timestamp=682.0)

### Ongoing Projects

  - [Jakub working on no-index page cleanup, expected to be a multi-week project](https://fathom.video/share/iCjVVTjnQtoV357jvPDcjzvRGhT8aXiV?tab=summary&timestamp=1473.0)
  - [Beau developing a tool to track visitors with high intent](https://fathom.video/share/iCjVVTjnQtoV357jvPDcjzvRGhT8aXiV?tab=summary&timestamp=1037.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Matt to ensure calibration articles are reviewed and feedback provided by end of week](https://fathom.video/share/iCjVVTjnQtoV357jvPDcjzvRGhT8aXiV?tab=summary&timestamp=201.0)
  - [Beau to adjust Webflow permissions for GrowthX team](https://fathom.video/share/iCjVVTjnQtoV357jvPDcjzvRGhT8aXiV?tab=summary&timestamp=682.0)
  - [GrowthX to continue work on no-index page cleanup, with updates expected next week](https://fathom.video/share/iCjVVTjnQtoV357jvPDcjzvRGhT8aXiV?tab=summary&timestamp=1497.0)
  - [Team to explore implementation of custom CTAs and banners in blog posts](https://fathom.video/share/iCjVVTjnQtoV357jvPDcjzvRGhT8aXiV?tab=summary&timestamp=1124.0)
  - [Consider creating a comprehensive "best data backup and archive tools" comparison article](https://fathom.video/share/iCjVVTjnQtoV357jvPDcjzvRGhT8aXiV?tab=summary&timestamp=1528.0)
